creator: dhorn2000@gmail.com
text:
Created attachment 244690
from /var/crash on 14-Alpha4 amd64

Environment:
FreeBSD freebsd14 14.0-ALPHA4 FreeBSD 14.0-ALPHA4 amd64 1400097 #0 stable/14-n265033-143371df7657-dirty: Mon Sep  4 23:27:03 EDT 2023     root@freebsd14:/usr/obj/usr/src/amd64.amd64/sys/GENERIC amd64
open-vm-tools-nox11-12.2.5_2,2
Name           : open-vm-tools-nox11
Version        : 12.2.5_2,2
Installed on   : Thu Sep  7 04:40:06 2023 EDT
Origin         : emulators/open-vm-tools
Architecture   : FreeBSD:14:amd64
Prefix         : /usr/local
Categories     : kld emulators
Licenses       : LGPL21
Maintainer     : garga@FreeBSD.org
WWW            : https://github.com/vmware/open-vm-tools
Comment        : Open VMware tools for FreeBSD VMware guests (without X11)
8 Cores
16GB RAM
2 VHDs (56GB + 64GB)
ESXi 8.0.1 Hypervisor

Steps to Reproduce:
1) Install 14-Alpha4 from ISO (amd64), other architectures untested.
2) pkg install open-vm-tools-nox11
3) shutdown -r now
4) Kernel panic during boot (before login)

During boot sequence, after going into multi-user mode and automatically running init scripts for open-vm-tools from /usr/local/etc/rc.d, kernel panic occurs.

panicmail_autosubmit was enabled, so including that data as well as attachment.

I suspect one of the kernel modules (vmmemctl.ko) from /usr/local/lib/vmware-tools/modules/drivers/ is causing the crash.

I have attached core.txt.0, panicmail.0, and info.0 from /var/crash on the impacted VM.

I am also attempting a clean build from latest ports tree of emulators/open-vm-tools to see if rebuilding helps.
